parts.title: Transcript
parts.annotated_transcript_html:
Speaker 1: You now have some time to look at the questions on your screen. Now answer the questions on your screen.

Speaker 2: Hello, Colvin Lettings, James Lansman speaking. How can I help?

Speaker 3: Oh, hello. I'm just calling to see what flats you currently have available to rent.

Speaker 2: Certainly, madam. We have a wide range of properties available, from studio flats to six-bedroom houses. What sort of place are you looking for?

Speaker 3: Well, a two-bed flat is what I'm really aiming for, but I'm open to other possibilities.

Speaker 2: Of course. Well, we'll see what we can do. What area are you thinking of?

Speaker 3: Either in the east side or the old port. I work in the centre, you see, but obviously, I don't really want to live in the centre. Who would? But somewhere fairly nearby with good transport links there would be ideal for me.

Speaker 2: I see. When are you thinking of moving?

Speaker 3: It would need to be sometime before the 13th of September, as that's when the contract on my old place runs out.

Speaker 2: So before the end of September, then?

Speaker 3: No, the middle of September, the 13th.

Speaker 2: Oh, right. Sorry. I thought you said the 30th. And do you have a set amount that you'd be willing to spend?

Speaker 3: Around £900 a month.

Speaker 2: Ah. Well, it might be difficult to find a two-bed flat in those areas for that price. Could you go up to £1200?

Speaker 3: Well, I suppose for the right place I could just about stretch to £1100.

Speaker 2: And how about looking at places a little smaller? Say, a one-bed instead of a two-bed?

Speaker 3: I suppose I could. Again, it would depend on the place.

Speaker 2: Of course, of course. Well, I'll tell you what I'll do. I'll have a quick look at what properties we've got available in those areas and give you a ring back. How does that sound?

Speaker 3: That'd be great, thanks.

Speaker 2: OK, I just need to take your name, if that's OK.

Speaker 3: Of course. It's Miss Beatrice Russell. That's Beatrice, B-E-A-T-R-I-C-E, Russell, R-U-S-S-E-L-L.

Speaker 2: OK, great. Thanks, Miss Russell. And your telephone number?

Speaker 3: 0774 0922 166.

Speaker 2: Let me just read that back to you. 0740922166.

Speaker 3: No, it's 007 at the beginning. 077...

Speaker 2: OK, got it. I'll give you a ring back shortly.

Speaker 3: That's great. Thanks. Bye.
